revision.text:
'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)
[[Category:Mino Province]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[ja:牟義都国造]]

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.

[[Category:Mino Province]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[category:most recent 500]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:牟義都国造]]

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}

== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.

=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}

== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱}}.== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

revision.text:
'''牟義都国造'''（むげつのくにのみやつこ・むげつこくぞう）は[[#支配領域|牟義都国]]（[[美濃国]]北中部）を支配した[[国造]]。
== 概要 == === 表記 === 『[[釈日本紀]]』の引用する『[[上宮記]]』に'''牟義都国造'''とある他、'''牟宜都君'''、'''身毛津君'''とも。
=== 祖先 === *『[[古事記]]』によると、[[景行天皇]]の子の[[大碓命]]が[[大根王|神大根王]]の娘の[[弟媛|弟比売]]と密通して誕生した'''[[押黒弟日子王]]'''が祖という。[[継体天皇]]の祖父も同国造の娘を娶ったという。
=== 氏族 === '''[[身毛氏]]'''（むげつうじ、[[カバネ|姓]]は[[君 (カバネ)|君]]）で、牟下・牟下都・牟下津・牟宜都・牟義・武義・牟宜津氏などとも。後に[[宿禰]]を賜るものもいた。
[[半布里戸籍]]により、[[鴨県主 (美濃)|鴨県主]]との婚姻関係が確認でき、同氏が持っていた水取としての職掌を継承して、[[元正天皇]]の美濃国の醴泉への行幸へ供奉したり、その醴泉を都に貢納するなどして、主水の役割を務めるようになった<ref>{{Cite book |和書|last= |first= |author= 関市教育委員会|authorlink= |coauthors= |year= 1996|title= 新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世|publisher= [[関市]]|location= |pages= 542 - 544|chapter= ムゲツ氏と内廷|id= |isbn= }}</ref>。
== 本拠 == 国造の本拠は不明であるが、牟義都国造'''伊自牟良君'''とあることから美濃国伊自良（現山県市旧伊自良村）を本拠とする説がある。
発掘調査により、7世紀後半の弥勒寺造営に先行する豪族居館と、奈良時代の[[郡衙]]に伴う[[正倉]]が設置されていたことが明らかになった。また前記『[[上宮記]]』（逸文）の伊自牟良を『[[和名抄]]』の山県郡出石郷に比定し、[[伊自良村]]（現山県市）に当てる説がある。『和名抄』の武芸郡有知郷ともされ、下有知の広域な条里制跡や重竹遺跡の大規模な古代集落跡が見られ、本拠として有力な候補地と見られるが、長良川右岸にある弥勒寺跡からは対岸にあたり、根拠は弱いと見る説もある<ref>{{Cite book |和書|last= |first= |author= 関市教育委員会|authorlink= |coauthors= |year= 1996|title= 新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世|publisher= 関市|location= |pages= 565 - 566|chapter= 有知郷|id= |isbn= }}</ref>。
=== 支配領域 === 国造の支配領域は当時'''牟義都国'''と呼ばれた地域、後の[[美濃国]][[武儀郡]]にあたり、現在の[[岐阜県]][[関市]]、[[美濃市]]、[[山県市]]に相当する。
== 氏神 == 不明。
=== 関連神社 === 『[[延喜式神名帳]]』に記載されないが、由緒に国造との関連が伝えられている。 *'''県神社'''（あがたじんじゃ） *:[[山県市]]に鎮座する神社。国造の伊自牟良君を県神として祀ったとされる<ref>「[http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ 県神社]」『岐阜県神社庁』</ref>。 *'''[[武芸八幡宮]]'''（むげはちまんぐう） *:[[関市]]に鎮座する神社。国造祖先の[[大碓命]]を祀ったのが始まりとされる。
=== 墓 ===
== 氏寺 == [[弥勒寺跡 (関市)|弥勒寺]]。
== 人物 == *'''[[伊自牟良君]]'''（いじむらのきみ） *:[[古墳時代]]の国造。久留比売命（[[乎非王]]の后）の父。 *'''身毛君丈夫'''（むげのきみ ますらお） *:[[雄略天皇]]の時代の人物。吉備弓削部虚空を[[吉備下道前津屋]]の下から呼び戻した。
== 子孫 == *'''[[身毛広]]'''（むげつのひろ） *:[[飛鳥時代]]の豪族。[[天武天皇|大海人皇子]]の舎人か。[[壬申の乱]]で活躍した。
== 関連項目 == *[[国造の一覧]]
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *『國史大辭典』(吉川弘文館) *坂本太郎・平野邦雄『日本古代氏族人名辞典』(吉川弘文館) *『日本史広辞典』(山川出版社) *『神道大辞典』(臨川書店)
{{DEFAULTSORT:むけつのくにのみやつこ}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:美濃国]] [[Category:国造]] [[Category:岐阜県の歴史]]

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国|qq=draft}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀|qq=draft}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|qq=draft}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|qq=draft}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|qq=draft}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|qq=draft}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡|qq=draft}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市|qq=draft}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市|qq=draft}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)
{{translated page|ja|牟義都国造|version=93692497|comment=Imported full ja history}}

[[Category:Mino Province]] [[Category:Kokuzō]] [[Category:History of Gifu Prefecture]] [[Category:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]]
}}

== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi District|qq=}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata|qq=}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata City|qq=}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki City|qq=}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

[[sn:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:牟義都国造]]

'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugetsu Province|qq=}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|ja|釈日本紀|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shaku Nihongi|qq=}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|ja|景行天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keikō|qq=}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|qq=}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|ja|継体天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Keitai|qq=}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|qq=}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|qq=}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genshō|qq=}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi District|qq=}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata|qq=}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata City|qq=}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki City|qq=}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|ja|宿禰|1=Sukune|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukune|qq=}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|qq=}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|qq=}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|ja|元正天皇|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Genshō|qq=}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District|ja|武儀郡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugi District|qq=}} in {{ill|Mino Province|ja|美濃国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino Province|qq=}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki|qq=}}, {{ill|Mino|ja|美濃市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mino|qq=}}, and {{ill|Yamagata|ja|山県市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata|qq=}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata City|ja|山県市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamagata City|qq=}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki City|ja|関市|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Seki City|qq=}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|ja|壬申の乱|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin War|qq=}}. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

revision.comment: Bot: replace {{ill}} with enwiki link via Wikidata
revision.text:
'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugetsu Province|qq=}}, located in the northern central part of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the [[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]] citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|qq=}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. Some members were later granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|qq=}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|qq=}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later [[:en:Mugi District, Gifu|Mugi District]] in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], including present-day [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki]], [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino]], and [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata]] in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata City]], it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]], it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} and distinguished himself during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata City]], it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]], it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} and distinguished himself during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

[[sn:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[en:Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko]] [[ja:牟義都国造]] [[Category:Kuni no miyatsuko]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis-->

revision.comment: Bot: remove invalid enwiki interwiki links
revision.text:
'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugetsu Province|qq=}}, located in the northern central part of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the [[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]] citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|qq=}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=}}. Some members were later granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|qq=}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|qq=}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later [[:en:Mugi District, Gifu|Mugi District]] in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], including present-day [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki]], [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino]], and [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata]] in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata City]], it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]], it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=}} and distinguished himself during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇}} and distinguished himself during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11570306]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:20, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugetsu Province|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牟義都国造}}, located in the northern central part of [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]].
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the [[:en:Shaku Nihongi|Shaku Nihongi]] citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|qq=}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]], {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子}}, a son of [[:en:Emperor Keikō|Emperor Keikō]], secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobime|qq=}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of [[:en:Emperor Keitai|Emperor Keitai]] married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to キミ (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title [[:en:Sukune|Sukune]]. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|qq=}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the [[:en:Empress Genshō|Empress Genshō]] on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later [[:en:Mugi District, Gifu|Mugi District]] in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], including present-day [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki]], [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino]], and [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata]] in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata City]], it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]], it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇}} and distinguished himself during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)

Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later [[:en:Mugi District, Gifu|Mugi District]] in [[:en:Mino Province|Mino Province]], including present-day [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki]], [[:en:Mino, Gifu|Mino]], and [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata]] in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in [[:en:Yamagata, Gifu|Yamagata City]], it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in [[:en:Seki, Gifu|Seki City]], it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Prince Oousu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|qq=|1=Ruins of Miroku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487421}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|qq=|1=King Hōfei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369801}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|qq=|1=Kibinoyugebe no Ozora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526057}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and distinguished himself during the [[:en:Jinshin War|Jinshin War]]. == See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)
