== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|en|Shaku Nihongi|WD=Q2710483|lt=Shaku Nihongi|1=Shaku Nihongi}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10867356}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|WD=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|WD=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobime|1=Otohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q119380628}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|WD=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to キミ (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|WD=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|1=Hanpurī Census|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11407005}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|WD=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District, Gifu|en|Mugi District, Gifu|WD=Q6932192|lt=Mugi District|1=Mugi District, Gifu}} in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|WD=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|WD=Q853835|lt=Mino|1=Mino, Gifu}}, and {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|WD=Q773311|lt=Yamagata|1=Yamagata, Gifu}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|WD=Q773311|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata, Gifu}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|WD=Q860647|lt=Seki City|1=Seki, Gifu}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11438297}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|1=Ruins of Miroku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11487421}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|1=King Hōfei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11369801}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|1=Kibinoyugebe no Ozora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55526057}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|WD=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=Kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2483673}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * ''Kokushi Daijiten'' (吉川弘文館) * Taro Sakamoto & Kunio Hirano, ''Nihon Kodai Shizoku Jinmei Jiten'' (吉川弘文館) * ''Nihonshi Hiro Jiten'' (山川出版社) * ''Shintō Daijiten'' (臨川書店)
{{translated page|ja|牟義都国造|version=93692497|comment=Imported full ja history}}
[[ja:牟義都国造]]

=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|qid=Q773311|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata, Gifu}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|qid=Q860647|lt=Seki City|1=Seki, Gifu}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438297}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|1=Ruins of Miroku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487421}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|1=King Hōfei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369801}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|1=Kibinoyugebe no Ozora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55526057}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=Kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2483673}}

revision.text:
'''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' (牟義都国造) refers to the ancient rulers of the {{ill|Mugetsu Province|ja|牟義都国|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mugetsu Province|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 牟義都国造|1=Mukizu no Kuni no Miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11570306}}, located in the northern central part of {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}.
== Overview ==
=== Terminology === The term '''Mugetsu Kokuzō''' is found in the excerpt from the {{ill|Shaku Nihongi|en|Shaku Nihongi|qid=Q2710483|lt=Shaku Nihongi|1=Shaku Nihongi}} citing the {{ill|Jōgūki|ja|上宮記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōgūki|1=Jōgūki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10867356}}, along with variations such as '''Mugitsu Kimi''' and '''Miketsu Kimi'''.
=== Ancestry === According to the {{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}, {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438297}}, a son of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Emperor Keikō|1=Emperor Keikō}}, secretly united with {{ill|Otobime|ja|弟媛|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Otobime|1=Otohime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q119380628}}, the daughter of {{ill|Kami Ōne no Ō|ja|神大根王|1=Kami Ōne|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne no Ō|13=User:Immanuelle/Kami Ōne|qq=}}, resulting in the birth of {{ill|Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja|押黒弟日子王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oshikuro Otobiko no Ō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, considered their progenitor. Additionally, it is said that the grandfather of {{ill|Emperor Keitai|en|Emperor Keitai|qid=Q349277|lt=Emperor Keitai|1=Emperor Keitai}} married a daughter of the same lineage.
=== Clan === The ruling clan was the '''Miketsu Clan''' (牟義都氏), also known as Mugitsu, Muketsu, or Mugitsu, with the hereditary title {{ill|Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi (kabane)|lt=Kimi|ja|君 (カバネ)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kimi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to キミ (カバネ)}}. Some members were later granted the title {{ill|Sukune|en|Sukune|qid=Q11455653|lt=Sukune|1=Sukune}}. Marriage records in the {{ill|Halfuri Register|ja|半布里戸籍|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Halfuri Register|1=Hanpurī Census|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11407005}} reveal ties to the {{ill|Kamo Agatanushi|ja|鴨県主 (美濃)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamo Agatanushi|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, suggesting that the Mugetsu Clan inherited the role of water management. They supported the {{ill|Empress Genshō|en|Empress Genshō|qid=Q235343|lt=Empress Genshō|1=Empress Genshō}} on her pilgrimage to the sacred springs of Mino and played roles in supplying the court with its resources.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=542-544}}</ref>
== Headquarters == The exact location of the Mugetsu Kokuzō's base is unknown, but one theory links it to Izura (伊自良) in present-day Yamagata City, Gifu Prefecture. Excavations have revealed remains of aristocratic residences from the late 7th century and warehouses associated with administrative facilities in the Nara period.<ref>{{Cite book |author=関市教育委員会 |year=1996 |title=新修 関市史 通史編 自然・原始・古代・中世 |publisher=関市 |pages=565-566}}</ref>
=== Territory === The domain of the Mugetsu Kokuzō, referred to as Mugetsu Province, corresponds to the later {{ill|Mugi District, Gifu|en|Mugi District, Gifu|qid=Q6932192|lt=Mugi District|1=Mugi District, Gifu}} in {{ill|Mino Province|en|Mino Province|qid=Q864156|lt=Mino Province|1=Mino Province}}, including present-day {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|qid=Q860647|lt=Seki|1=Seki, Gifu}}, {{ill|Mino, Gifu|en|Mino, Gifu|qid=Q853835|lt=Mino|1=Mino, Gifu}}, and {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|qid=Q773311|lt=Yamagata|1=Yamagata, Gifu}} in Gifu Prefecture.
== Shrine == The clan's tutelary shrine remains unclear.
=== Related Shrines === Though not recorded in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, certain shrines are traditionally linked to the Mugetsu Kokuzō: *'''Agata Shrine''' (県神社): Located in {{ill|Yamagata, Gifu|en|Yamagata, Gifu|qid=Q773311|lt=Yamagata City|1=Yamagata, Gifu}}, it is said to enshrine Izimura no Kimi, a Mugetsu Kokuzō.<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://www.gifu-jinjacho.jp/syosai.php?shrno=1773&shrname=★県神社★ |title=Agata Shrine |publisher=岐阜県神社庁}}</ref> *'''Muge Hachiman Shrine''' (武芸八幡宮): Situated in {{ill|Seki, Gifu|en|Seki, Gifu|qid=Q860647|lt=Seki City|1=Seki, Gifu}}, it is believed to enshrine {{ill|Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja|大碓命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuchi no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大碓皇子|1=Ōusu-no-Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11438297}}.
== Associated Temple == The clan's temple is {{ill|Miroku-ji Temple|ja|弥勒寺跡 (関市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miroku-ji Temple|1=Ruins of Miroku-ji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11487421}}.
== Notable Figures == *'''Izimura no Kimi''' (伊自牟良君): A Kokuzō of the Kofun period and the father of {{ill|Kurubime|ja|久留比売命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kurubime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, the consort of {{ill|Ōhobi no Ō|ja|乎非王|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōhobi no Ō|1=King Hōfei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11369801}}. *'''Muge Kimi Masurao''' (身毛君丈夫): Active during Emperor Yūryaku's reign, he summoned {{ill|Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|ja|吉備弓削部虚空|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi no Yugobe no Kokū|1=Kibinoyugebe no Ozora|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q55526057}} back to court. *'''Mugetsu no Hiro''' (身毛広): A prominent figure in the Asuka period who likely served {{ill|Prince Ōama|ja|大海人皇子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prince Ōama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} and distinguished himself during the {{ill|Jinshin War|en|Jinshin War|qid=Q2378252|lt=Jinshin War|1=Jinshin War}}.
== See Also == * {{ill|List of Kokuzō|ja|国造の一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Kokuzō|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 国造|1=Kuni no miyatsuko|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2483673}}
